lede: A look at March 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Albert Einstein and Nicolas Anelka, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Nihonium
events.desc: A naming ceremony for the chemical element nihonium takes place in Tokyo, with then Crown Prince Naruhito in attendance.

events.title: Escondida
events.desc: Escondida in Chile's Atacama Desert – which was to become the worlds most productive copper mine – is officially inaugurated.

events.title: Johnson South Reef skirmish
events.desc: In the Johnson South Reef Skirmish Chinese forces defeat Vietnamese forces in an altercation over control of one of the Spratly Islands.

events.title: Korean War
events.desc: Korean War: United Nations troops recapture Seoul for the second time.

events.title: Royal Air Force
events.desc: The R.A.F. drop the Grand Slam bomb in action for the first time, on a railway viaduct near Bielefeld, Germany.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: The Holocaust: The liquidation of the Kraków Ghetto is completed.

events.title: Alam Ara
events.desc: Alam Ara, India's first talking film, is released.

events.title: The Mikado
events.desc: The Mikado, a light opera by W. S. Gilbert and Arthur Sullivan, receives its first public performance at the Savoy Theatre in London.

events.title: Petite messe solennelle
events.desc: Rossini's Petite messe solennelle is first performed, by twelve singers, two pianists and a harmonium player in a mansion in Paris.

events.title: Thirty Years' War
events.desc: Thirty Years' War: Bavaria, Cologne, France and Sweden sign the Truce of Ulm.

births.name: Albert Einstein
births.line: German-American physicist, academic and Nobel Prize laureate

births.name: Aamir Khan
births.line: Indian film actor, producer, and director

deaths.text: William Alfred Fowler, American physicist and astronomer, Nobel Prize laureate
Zita of Bourbon-Parma, Empress of Austria and Queen of Hungary
Mohammad Hatta, Indonesian politician, 3rd Prime Minister of Indonesia

observances.text: Nanakshahi New Year, first day of the month of Chet (Sikhism)
